examples: An autotrophic, unicellular, green alga presents the minimum of specialization.
The Earth was just over 2 billion years old, and home only to unicellular life-forms.
Think again. This unicellular diatom (diatom Arachnoidiscus), a type of algae, reveals an intricate rainbow pattern under 40x magnification.
According to the news, the unicellular microorganisms are rod-shaped and nourish themselves by photosynthesis.
By searching for genetic sequences similar to regA in the burgeoning databases of genes that now exist, they found one in a unicellular alga called Chlamydomonas reinhardtii.
